title: Dataset of intracranial EEG during cortical stimulation evoking visual effects

affiliations.name: Physics Department University of Bucharest  Bucharest Romania
Physics Department University of Bucharest  Bucharest Romania
Epilepsy Monitoring Unit, Neurology Department Emergency University Hospital Bucharest  Bucharest Romania
Epilepsy Monitoring Unit, Neurology Department Emergency University Hospital Bucharest  Bucharest Romania
Neurology Department, Faculty of Medicine Carol Davila University of Medicine and Pharmacy Bucharest  Bucharest Romania

description: This dataset comprises intracranial EEG recordings from 22 patients undergoing stereo-EEG evaluation for drug-resistant epilepsy, capturing neural responses to 115 high-frequency stimulation events that evoked visual hallucinations. Each recording contains 21 seconds of iEEG data (8 seconds pre-stimulation, up to 5 seconds stimulation, and 8 seconds post-stimulation) with bipolar stimulation parameters and clinical effect classifications across 14 visual symptom categories. The dataset enables investigation of brain networks underlying cortical visual effects through alternating polarity stimulation protocols.
methods_description: High-frequency bipolar intracranial stimulations were delivered using alternating polarity biphasic pulses (1 ms duration) at 43.2 Hz or 50 Hz with current intensities between 0.25-3 mA for up to 5 seconds. iEEG recordings were acquired for 21 seconds per stimulation event (8 seconds pre-stimulation, stimulation period, and 8 seconds post-stimulation). Stimulation contact pairs, current intensity levels, and evoked clinical effects were documented in event files.
